evidence: Section 3.4 states: "The most reliable of those [variable importance measures] is based on the decrease of prediction accuracy resulting from random attribute value permutation, estimated using out-of-bag training instances. This measure is used in the experiments reported in this article." The paper cites Strobl et al. 2007 on bias in random forest variable importance and deliberately avoids impurity importance. Figure 6 ranks attributes by permutation importance, but these rankings are secondary to the paper's main conclusions about algorithm performance (RF and xgboost deliver best predictive performance with AUC 0.86-0.98).
